regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 25, 2026, features light east-southeast winds at 3 knots in San Juan with partly cloudy skies and a 58 percent chance of scattered showers. Conditions will become breezier with east winds 10-12 knots through March 27, accompanied by increasing rain showers and thunderstorms, especially near San Juan and St. Thomas. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor localized thunderstorm activity, which may bring gusty winds and brief heavy downpours, reducing visibility and creating choppy seas near exposed anchorages like St. Maarten's Great Bay. Cruise passengers are advised to carry rain gear and expect intermittent wet weather while ashore, particularly at San Juan and St. Croix, but no marine warnings are currently in effect.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 25, 2026: Today’s conditions in Cozumel show light northeast winds at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain expected, ideal for open-water activities and port calls. Coastal waters remain calm; yachts and fishing vessels should experience smooth seas with ENE winds increasing slightly to 13 knots over the next two days. Expect overcast skies tomorrow in Cozumel and Costa Maya with a very low rain chance, but by day three, scattered rain showers may develop—monitor local forecasts for Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth as similar conditions spread. Passengers should plan outdoor excursions today and tomorrow but prepare for possible wet weather by midweek; captains should secure light gear and remain vigilant for localized showers, particularly nearshore.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 25, 2026: Current conditions in Nassau are clear with light northeast winds at 5 knots and a mild 64 degrees. Expect mostly fair weather today with ENE winds increasing to 10 knots and highs near 79 degrees. Over the next three days, light winds from the northeast to east will prevail with mostly overcast skies and isolated drizzle by day three; rain chances remain below 10 percent throughout. Mariners and captains operating near Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos should anticipate calm seas with no advisories; fishing vessels should take advantage of stable conditions but remain alert for light drizzle later in the week that may reduce visibility in some areas.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean briefing for March 25, 2026: Aruba reports steady east winds 11 knots with partly cloudy skies and minimal rain chances today. Expect increasing easterly winds 15-18 knots over the next three days with mostly overcast conditions and isolated showers below 10 percent probability. Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should see similar stable trade wind patterns with no marine wind warnings in effect. Mariners operating yachts or fishing vessels should secure light gear and monitor for gusts as winds build; anchorage conditions remain favorable with moderate seas. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities with sun protection but minimal weather disruption.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today feature northeast winds sustained near 17 knots with overcast skies and a 30 percent chance of rain at Grand Cayman. Expect persistent rain showers through the next three days, with winds holding steady between 14 and 19 knots from the northeast to east-northeast and rain probabilities ranging from 48 to 90 percent. Mariners should prepare for choppy seas and reduced visibility, especially near Grand Cayman and the Belize City area; small craft operators should exercise caution as winds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Passengers are advised to carry rain gear and plan for possible itinerary adjustments at Roatan, Cozumel, and Costa Maya due to scattered showers and unsettled weather.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 25, 2026: Winds remain steady from the east-northeast at 16-17 knots with partly cloudy to overcast skies and minimal rain chances across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, and neighboring ports. Seas are moderate and stable, no Small Craft Advisories or Gale Warnings expected. Yacht captains should maintain caution entering exposed anchorages in Antigua and St. Kitts where easterly winds can funnel and increase chop. Cruise passengers can expect comfortable temperatures near 80-82°F with mostly dry conditions, but overcast skies may limit sun exposure.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and surrounding ports are currently overcast with light WNW winds near 1 knot and temperatures around 62°F. Expect a light westerly breeze to increase to 10-12 knots over the next two days, with drizzle today and fog developing tomorrow, primarily affecting visibility near Los Angeles/Long Beach harbors and Catalina Island approaches. No rain is expected, but mariners should plan for reduced visibility and damp decks; maintain cautious navigation in fog-prone areas such as Marina del Rey. By day three, winds shift slightly WSW at 7 knots with overcast skies and warming to low 80s, favorable for smooth operations and passenger comfort.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions are stable with light northwest winds at 1 knot and overcast skies this morning, temperatures near 59°F and no precipitation expected. Fog will develop the next two days with light WSW winds at 7 knots, reducing visibility mostly around Ensenada and Punta Banda; mariners should maintain vigilant navigation and use radar or GPS when entering or leaving port. By day three, temperatures rise to the low 80s with continued overcast and minimal rain chance, posing no significant weather threats. Fishing and yacht captains should expect calm seas and light winds, but plan for limited visibility in early mornings near Islas Todos Santos.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and nearby ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will see mild northwest winds steady at 12-13 knots with partly cloudy skies today and through March 27. No precipitation expected, and seas should remain moderate, favoring smooth navigation and stable anchorage conditions. Yacht and fishing captains can expect consistent wind for trolling or light sail, but remain alert for increasing cloud cover by day three which may reduce visibility. Passengers should enjoy comfortable temperatures near 74-76°F with minimal weather disruptions for shore excursions.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and nearby ports including Topolobampo and Altata will experience light winds from the northwest to southwest at 2 to 9 knots through March 25-27, with partly cloudy skies and no significant rain threat. Seas are expected to remain calm, well below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Yacht and fishing captains should find favorable conditions for navigation and anchoring, particularly in the well-protected Altata and Topolobampo anchorages. Cruise passengers can expect comfortable temperatures near 77-78°F with minimal weather disruption.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala will experience stable marine conditions today with temperatures near 80°F and light westerly winds at 8 to 9 knots under persistent overcast skies. No rain or marine wind warnings are expected through the next three days, supporting safe navigation and comfortable anchorage. Fishing captains should anticipate calm seas and steady winds ideal for offshore activities, while yacht operators can expect smooth maneuvering in all local harbors. Passengers should prepare for mild, overcast weather with no precipitation, making outdoor excursions enjoyable but with limited direct sunlight.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 25, 2026: Barcelona currently clear at 53°F with light WNW winds 2 knots and no rain. Expect increasing SW winds to 8 knots with overcast skies later today and dry conditions, easing anchorage operations at Barcelona and nearby ports. By March 26, southerly winds will strengthen to 11 knots with a 35 percent chance of rain showers, requiring caution for small craft and fishing vessels around Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and Ibiza. Conditions improve slightly on March 27 with overcast skies and lighter SW winds near 9 knots; maintain vigilance for localized showers especially near Malaga and Cadiz. Passengers should plan outdoor activities for today and carry rain gear midweek; captains should monitor forecasts for shifting winds and moderate seas in port approaches.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean conditions today feature light NNE winds near 1 knot and clear skies across Naples with temperatures around 55°F. Expect a mild increase in winds from the WSW up to 9 knots with overcast skies tomorrow, remaining dry. On March 26, wind speeds rise to 20 knots WSW accompanied by scattered rain showers around Naples and nearby ports including Civitavecchia and Livorno; a Small Craft Advisory may be prudent for smaller vessels in these areas. By March 27, winds ease to 7 knots from the north with overcast conditions and minimal rain chances. Yacht and fishing captains should secure gear ahead of Tuesday’s increasing winds and showers, while cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities for today or Monday morning before wet weather arrives.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean outlook for March 25, 2026 is marked by variable conditions. Athens/Piraeus currently holds a mild 56°F with light northerly winds at 4 knots under overcast skies and a 30% chance of showers. Expect strengthening northerly winds to 13 knots with steady rain and an 88% chance today, easing tomorrow with southerly 7-knot winds and dry, overcast conditions. Thunderstorms may return on day three with southwest winds near 12 knots and a 36% rain probability. Mariners should monitor rain and thunderstorms near Athens, Rhodes, and Cyprus for possible reduced visibility and gusty squalls; small craft operators should exercise caution in exposed anchorages. Passengers should carry rain gear and be prepared for occasional wet decks, especially in Piraeus and Santorini.
